_source: International Residential Code, carpentry textbooks, Framing practices guides
_note: Long-tail carpentry knowledge. Framing techniques, lumber sizing, code requirements, structural principles.

nominal_vs_actual.reason: Nominal size is rough-cut; planing and drying reduces to actual

grades.select_structural: Highest grade; fewest knots; strongest; headers, beams
grades.no1_no2: Standard framing grade; studs, joists, rafters; most economical
grades.stud_grade: Vertical use only; straightness important; not for joists/rafters

species.southern_pine: Dense; strong; pressure-treats well; southeast US

components.top_plate: Two 2x members on top; laps at corners for strength; ties walls together
components.studs: Vertical members; 16" or 24" on-center (o.c.); support loads
components.headers: Over openings (doors, windows); carries load; sized for span
components.cripple_studs: Short studs above/below openings; maintain stud spacing
components.king_stud: Full-height stud beside opening; supports header
components.jack_stud: Trimmer; under header; supports vertical load; same depth as header

stud_spacing.16_inch_oc: Standard; stronger; more lumber; better for drywall (less sag)
stud_spacing.24_inch_oc: Allowed for 1-story or 2nd floor; saves lumber; requires 5/8" drywall
stud_spacing.code: IRC allows 24" o.c. for 2x4 studs in 1-story or 2nd floor; 16" o.c. for 2x3; verify local

header_sizing.rule_of_thumb: Span in feet ÷ 2 = depth in inches for typical loads; 8 ft span = 2x8 header
header_sizing.proper_method: Calculate load; use span tables; factors: snow load, floor loads, span
header_sizing.bearing: Header bears on jack studs; header length = opening + 2× jack stud width (3")

joist_sizing.factors: Span, spacing (12"/16"/24" o.c.), species, grade, load (live + dead)
joist_sizing.span_tables: IRC Table R502.3.1; must consult for code compliance

examples_16_oc.2x8_no2_spf: 12 ft span max for 40 psf live load

joist_sizing.rim_joist: Joist-depth member at perimeter; boxes in floor frame; same depth as joists

bridging.purpose: Prevent joist rotation; distribute loads; stiffen floor

types.cross_bridging: Diagonal wood or metal; X-pattern between joists; 8 ft o.c. rows
types.solid_blocking: Full-depth solid 2x between joists; strongest; more labor
types.continuous_blocking: Row of solid blocking at mid-span for >8 ft spans

bridging.code: Required for joists >12" deep or spanning >8 ft (varies by jurisdiction)

cantilevers.rule: Cantilever up to 1/4 of back span; 8 ft back span allows 2 ft cantilever
cantilevers.code: IRC R502.3.3; limit 2x8 cantilever to 24" max; 2x10 to 30"; back span must be 2× cantilever min
cantilevers.framing: Joists run perpendicular; extend beyond beam; block between joists at beam

subfloor.thickness: 23/32" (3/4") OSB or plywood; 16" o.c. joists; 19/32" for 24" o.c. not recommended
subfloor.tongue_and_groove: T&G edges; no blocking needed on edges; glue and screw; prevents squeaks
subfloor.fastening: Glue (PL400 or equivalent) + screws or ring-shank nails; 6" edges, 12" field
subfloor.expansion: 1/8" gap at edges for expansion; caulk before subfloor to prevent squeak

rafter_basics.rise_run: Rise = vertical change in inches per foot of run; 6/12 pitch = 6" rise per 12" run
rafter_basics.common_rafter: Runs from ridge to top plate; perpendicular to ridge
rafter_basics.hip_rafter: 45° from corner; connects ridge to outside corner; longer than common
rafter_basics.valley_rafter: Inside corner; connects two roof sections; longer than common
rafter_basics.jack_rafter: Runs from hip/valley to top plate or ridge; shorter than common

rafter_sizing.factors: Span (horizontal projection), spacing, species, grade, snow load, dead load
rafter_sizing.span_tables: IRC Table R802.4.1; based on ground snow load

roof_pitch.low_slope: <4/12; requires membrane roofing or metal; prone to leaks with shingles
roof_pitch.standard: 4/12 to 9/12; shingles, metal, tile; 6/12 most common
roof_pitch.walkable: 6/12 and under walkable with caution; >8/12 needs staging/harness

truss_vs_stick.stick_framing: Site-built rafters + ridge beam; allows attic space/cathedral ceiling; more labor; traditional
truss_vs_stick.trusses: Pre-engineered; stronger; faster; cheaper; web members occupy attic; can't modify
truss_vs_stick.truss_spacing: 24" o.c. typical; engineered for span and load; must follow truss drawings exactly

birdsmouth_cut.definition: Notch in rafter where it sits on top plate; provides bearing
birdsmouth_cut.depth: Seat cut depth ≤1/3 rafter depth; deeper weakens rafter; 2x6 rafter = 1.75" max seat cut
birdsmouth_cut.layout: Plumb cut (vertical) + seat cut (horizontal); angles determined by pitch

nails.common: Thick shank; large head; framing; 16d (3.5") for 2x to 2x; 8d (2.5") for sheathing
nails.box: Thinner shank than common; less splitting; finish work
nails.sinker: Coated; tapered head; nail guns; 16d sinker = 3.25" (shorter than common 16d)
nails.ring_shank: Spiral/ring grooves; high withdrawal resistance; subfloor, sheathing; prevent pops/squeaks

screws.structural: GRK, Spax, FastenMaster; high shear/withdrawal; no pre-drill; replace bolts for some applications
screws.drywall_screws: Not for structural; brittle; shear easily; fine thread for wood studs

hangers_and_hardware.joist_hanger: Metal bracket; suspends joist from beam/ledger; use hanger-specific nails (10d × 1.5")
hangers_and_hardware.hurricane_ties: Strap tie rafter to top plate; prevents uplift in high wind; required in coastal zones
hangers_and_hardware.hold_downs: Anchor shear wall to foundation; prevents overturning; requires engineered design for high shear
hangers_and_hardware.simpson_strong_tie: Brand; catalog with capacities; specify model for inspections; use correct fasteners

bearing.minimum_bearing: 1.5" for sawn lumber on wood; 3" on masonry/concrete
bearing.bearing_area: Must be sufficient for load; crushing perpendicular to grain = 625 psi for most species

notching_joists.top_edge: No notches in middle 1/3 span; outer 1/3 max depth = 1/6 joist depth
notching_joists.bottom_edge: Max depth = 1/6 joist depth; outer 1/3 only
notching_joists.holes: Max diameter = 1/3 depth; min 2" from edge; not in middle 1/3

fire_blocking.purpose: Prevent vertical fire spread in concealed spaces
fire_blocking.locations: Between floors; at ceiling/roof; vertical stud runs >10 ft; soffits, drops
fire_blocking.material: 2x4 blocking, 3/4" plywood, gypsum board, fire-rated material

shear_walls.purpose: Resist lateral loads (wind, seismic); braced walls prevent racking
shear_walls.bracing_methods: Structural sheathing (most common), diagonal let-in bracing, portal frames
shear_walls.amount: Based on seismic/wind zone; IRC prescriptive tables; engineered for high-risk areas
shear_walls.fastening: Edge nailing 4-6" o.c.; field 12" o.c.; specific for shear rating

layout.crown_up: Dimensional lumber has crown (arch); install crown up in joists/rafters so sag doesn't increase deflection
layout.layout_marks: X = center on mark; O = stud/joist at mark; measure from same end consistently
layout.story_pole: Mark all vertical measurements on single board; ensures consistency; faster than measuring each stud

squaring.3_4_5_triangle: 3 ft + 4 ft = 5 ft hypotenuse if square; scale up: 6-8-10, 9-12-15
squaring.diagonal_method: Measure corner to corner both ways; equal = square; adjust until match
squaring.framing_square: Check for square; 16" and 24" markings for stud layout
